description: In ed25519_tests::sign_parses_64_byte_signature, the mock backend returns arbitrary bytes ([42u8; 64]) and the test checks that the round-trip through ed25519::Signature::from_slice then to_bytes() reproduces the input. The ed25519 crate accepts any 64-byte slice without cryptographic validation, so this test only exercises byte identity, not that the adapter produces a valid signature. Similarly rsa_tests::sign_parses_signature checks only length. What goes wrong: the tests pass even if the adapter is wired to the wrong byte path (e.g., passing the message instead of the signature). Tests should use a real known-good key fixture with a pre-computed signature from an external oracle (e.g., OpenSSL or pyca/cryptography) to verify end-to-end correctness.
title: Test oracle tests adapter not cryptographic validity
